description: Internal namespace patterns, private registry indicators, and dependency-confusion heuristics. Any public-registry package matching one of these patterns is treated as a shadowing attack until proven otherwise.

namespaces.description: Replace 'yourco' with your organization's npm scope; ensure .npmrc routes the scope to the internal registry via `@yourco:registry=https://npm.internal.example.com/`.
Replace with your organization's Java group ID; the resolver must prefer the internal repository before Maven Central.
Replace with your organization's PyPI prefix; configure pip.conf with --extra-index-url and prefer the internal index.
Underscore variant; PyPI normalizes hyphens and underscores so attackers can publish either form.
Replace with your organization's Go module path; set GOPRIVATE=github.com/yourco/* so the public proxy is bypassed for internal modules.
Replace with your organization's crates prefix; cargo by default has no scopes, so prefix conventions matter.

heuristics.description: A public registry contains a package with the same name as an internal one. Treat as confusion attack regardless of version.
Internal-namespace package was first published to the public registry within the last 90 days.
Public package has a version dramatically higher than the internal one (e.g., 99.99.99) to win resolution.
Project uses an internal-namespace package but has no .npmrc / pip.conf / GOPRIVATE override pinning that namespace to an internal source.
Lockfile references a package by name only; no explicit registry URL pinning.
Code published to a public repo references an internal-namespace package without configuring the public consumer to resolve it.

known_incidents.name: Birsan dependency confusion (2021)
known_incidents.summary: Researcher Alex Birsan published lookalike packages for internal namespaces of Apple, Microsoft, Tesla, Yelp and 35+ other companies; received code execution callbacks from corporate networks.

known_incidents.name: Amazon AWS-CLI confusion (2022)
known_incidents.summary: Lookalike PyPI uploads targeted internal AWS-CLI namespaces; demonstrated the pattern extends to enterprise CLI tooling.
